raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 524 would prohibit discrimination against employees based on their status as a family caregiver.",
    "Assembly Bill 518 would make California leave laws more accessible to LGBTQ+ Californians and others who care for extended or chosen family.",
    "Roughly 60 percent of two-parent households with children under age 18 have both parents working.",
    "More than 1 in 6 Americans working full-time or part-time report assisting with the care of an elderly or disabled family member, relative, or friend.",
    "More than 1 in 12 employed adults are caring for both children and elderly or disabled adults.",
    "Working mothers and pregnant people are most likely to experience discrimination based on family caregiver status.",
    "Low-wage earners and people of color are disproportionately impacted by discrimination based on family caregiver status.",
    "California has less access to affordable child care than many other states.",
    "Women, who are more likely to be family caregivers, disproportionately work in low-wage jobs where it is common to be fired or retaliated against based on caregiver status.",
    "Currently, most California workers pay into the Paid Family Leave program through automatic paycheck deductions.",
    "Those with chosen family cannot currently access California's Paid Family Leave program when they need time away from work to care for their loved ones."
  ]
}
```
